dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for an unconscious 83-year-old female with a reported fainting episode.
EMS is responding to a report of an unresponsive 83-year-old female at the residence.
The patient was found unresponsive at 07:29 with a Glasgow Coma Scale of 3.
She was unresponsive to painful stimuli per GCS assessment.
Eyes did not open to verbal or physical stimuli.
No verbal response was observed.
No motor movement occurred with painful stimuli.
Airway is patent, and the patient has not required any assistance with breathing.
Respirations are 12, with an oxygen saturation of 98% on room air.
Pulse is 113, blood pressure is 168/91, and skin is cool and clammy.
From family, the patient has a history of insulin-dependent diabetes, hypertension, and hyperlipidemia.
Patient was unresponsive with no pain reported according to family and EMS observation.
Last food and drink are unknown, but hypoglycemic symptoms were observed initially.
At 07:30, the pulse was 113 beats per minute.
Respiratory rate was 12 at the time of scene arrival.
Blood pressure was 168/91 mmHg with normal initial EKG findings.
Glucose level was 54 mg/dL, indicating hypoglycemia.
Oxygen saturation was 98% on room air at this time.
EKG showed sinus rhythm during the initial monitoring.
At 07:35, an IV was successfully started in an extremity.
Dextrose 10% was given intravenously at 07:57 as part of the hypoglycemia protocol.
By 07:51, pulse was 101, respiratory rate 14, and blood pressure 143/75.
Respiratory rate was 14 at this assessment time with no significant distress.
Blood pressure was 143/75 at that time with no change in pattern.
Glucose level remained at 54, still indicating hypoglycemia.
Oxygen saturation was 100% at that time.
EKG now showed sinus tachycardia after initial treatment.
By 08:16, the glucose had increased to 273, pulse was 113, and blood pressure was 168/106.
Respiratory rate rose to 16, and breathing remained regular.
Oxygen saturation was 99% at 08:22.
EKG continued to show sinus tachycardia post-reassessment.
Blood pressure was noted as 168/106 at 08:16.
At 08:22, pulse was 104, respiratory rate 16, and blood pressure 165/109.
At 08:27, we contacted medical control to confirm ongoing care.
Transport destination was decided due to altered mental status and chronic conditions.
Patient's mental status improved to alert and oriented to 2 of 4, but she remained confused.
5-point restraints were applied for safety during the transport.
Patient was moved using a sheet assist to the stretcher for safer transport.
Hypoglycemia protocol was correctly followed per documented interventions and medical control.
ER personnel were informed of the protocols followed and the mental status change.
Unit three, patient is en route and protocol is complete.
